title: MATLAB: Working with Functions
summary: This page demonstrates MATLAB techniques for plotting signal transformations, specifically g(−t+1) and h(t) = g(2t+1) + g(−t+1), illustrating reflection and time-shifting operations. It also introduces the topic of numerical integration in MATLAB for estimating signal energy, noting that many difficult integrals can be accurately approximated using numerical methods.

figures.caption: Plot of h(t) = g(2t + 1) + g(-t + 1) over the interval -2 ≤ t ≤ 2, showing the superposition of a time-scaled/shifted and a time-reversed/shifted version of g(t).
figures.description: The figure plots h(t) versus t over the interval -2 to 2, with the horizontal axis labeled t and the vertical axis labeled h(t). The waveform is the sum of two transformed versions of g: a time-scaled and shifted term g(2t+1) and a time-reversed and shifted term g(-t+1), producing noticeable jumps near t=-0.5 and t=1 and a large peak around t=1. Students should notice how superposition combines these transformed signals and how scaling, shifting, and reversal change where features appear on the t-axis.
